title: physospermum aquilegiifolium var. cornubiense
intro: Physospermum aquilegiifolium var. cornubiense, also known as the Cornish bladderwort, is a rare and endemic plant species found in the southwestern part of England. It belongs to the family Berberidaceae and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and infections. In herbal preparations, Physospermum aquilegiifolium var. cornubiense can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical applications, although its use is largely anecdotal and requires further research for its efficacy and safety.
